_README: Real passenger-ferry corridors. The engine only ever offers a ferry leg that appears here — no formula-invented boats. port_a/port_b are actual ferry terminals (lat/lng ~1km); duration_h = typical published crossing; frequency_per_day = typical departures in normal season (0.5 = every other day); price_usd_lo/hi = adult one-way foot-passenger fare band in USD; seasonal = service much thinner or absent off-season. Every entry carries operators + a price_source checked on price_asof. Compiled from operator/aggregator pages 2026-07; regenerate/extend by research, not by formula.

corridors.name: Algeciras – Ceuta
corridors.notes: 60 min fast ferry / 90 min conventional. Three operators combine for ~112 weekly sailing options.

corridors.name: Algeciras – Tanger Med
corridors.notes: Busiest Strait of Gibraltar crossing; up to 53 sailings/week from FRS alone.

corridors.name: Ancona – Igoumenitsa
corridors.price_basis: adult foot passenger one-way, reclining seat
corridors.notes: Overnight Adriatic crossing, often continuing to Patras (21-25h total). Cabins cost substantially more (avg cabin price cited near $800+).

corridors.name: Aomori – Hakodate
corridors.notes: Shortest Honshu-Hokkaido sea crossing, budget alternative to the Shinkansen. Two operators combine for 14 daily round trips.

corridors.name: Auckland – Waiheke Island
corridors.notes: Passenger-only (vehicles go via a separate barge operator, SeaLink). Sailings from NZD31 one-way quoted directly; up to 22 sailings/day in peak season.

corridors.name: Bar Harbor – Yarmouth (Nova Scotia)
corridors.notes: Seasonal (roughly May/June-Oct only). Adult US$115, senior (60+) US$110, quoted directly by operator.

corridors.name: Barcelona – Palma de Mallorca
corridors.notes: Three operators, mostly overnight sailings. Cheapest advance fares from EUR14-15.

corridors.name: Bari – Durrës
corridors.notes: Daily year-round sailing; deck fare from EUR45, early-booking discounts up to 50%.

corridors.name: Buenos Aires – Colonia del Sacramento
corridors.notes: Two competing operators; Colonia Express generally undercuts Buquebus. No vehicles carried on passenger fast ferries.

corridors.name: Buenos Aires – Montevideo
corridors.notes: 5 fare classes from High Speed economy to First Class. Fastest crossing 2.5h, conventional up to 4h. No vehicles carried.

corridors.name: Busan – Fukuoka (Hakata)
corridors.notes: IMPORTANT: the JR Kyushu Beetle jetfoil (the ~3h high-speed option) was PERMANENTLY DISCONTINUED in Dec 2024 due to hull-crack safety concerns -- do not resurrect it. Camellia Line's overnight ferry (~9h, departs ~22:00) is now the only direct passenger service on this corridor.

corridors.name: Cádiz – Las Palmas (Canaries)
corridors.price_basis: adult foot passenger one-way, reclining seat
corridors.notes: LOWER CONFIDENCE: multi-day mainland-to-Canaries crossing, 2-3x/week, priced only in EUR90-120 'from' terms; a private cabin costs substantially more (family-of-4-with-car packages cited at EUR400-600).

corridors.name: Cape May – Lewes
corridors.notes: Confirmed directly by operator: $8 off-season / $10 in-season one-way; seniors 62+ pay less. Delaware Bay crossing.

corridors.name: Cebu – Tagbilaran (Bohol)
corridors.notes: High-speed passenger-only fastcraft, PHP500 (open-air/tourist) to PHP1000 (business). 16 daily trips each way.

corridors.name: Civitavecchia (Rome) – Olbia
corridors.notes: Day crossings ~7h50-9h, night ~9h; fares from ~EUR 23. Daily-sailing count across Tirrenia/Grimaldi/Moby unverified; 2/day is an in-season estimate.

corridors.name: Copenhagen – Oslo
corridors.notes: Classic overnight cruise-ferry, cabin effectively mandatory. One round-trip sailing per day.

corridors.name: Dakar – Ziguinchor
corridors.notes: Overnight Casamance run, only 2 sailings/week (Tue/Fri from Dakar). Resident fare from XOF5,000, non-resident from XOF15,900, private cabins from XOF35,000.

corridors.name: Dar es Salaam – Zanzibar
corridors.notes: Non-resident USD pricing quoted by operator directly (residents pay far less in TZS). Four classes: Economy/Tourist/Business/Royal/VIP.

corridors.name: Don Sak – Koh Samui
corridors.notes: THB180 base fare, hourly departures 5am-7pm. Very cheap per-km for a vehicle-capable RoRo ferry -- useful low-price-band anchor for the Thai Gulf.

corridors.name: Dover – Calais
corridors.notes: GBP32-134 range across operators/dates per Direct Ferries/AFerry; used typical (not extreme-peak) band. Busiest short-strait corridor in Europe.

corridors.name: Dubrovnik – Korčula
corridors.notes: Multiple catamaran/ferry operators; frequency drops off-season. Fare EUR9.82-30 depending on operator/vessel type.

corridors.name: Fishguard – Rosslare
corridors.notes: 13 sailings/week. Pembroke Dock-Rosslare (Irish Ferries) is a nearby alternative not separately priced here.

corridors.name: Frederikshavn – Gothenburg
corridors.notes: 26 sailings/week. Cheap advance fares from ~EUR21.

corridors.name: Galveston – Port Bolivar
corridors.notes: Free 24/7 TxDOT highway ferry (part of Highway 87), no fare of any kind. ~18-min crossing, up to 7 boats running at peak.

corridors.name: Genoa – Olbia
corridors.notes: Overnight Sardinia mainland link; cheapest deck fares from ~EUR40.

corridors.name: Harwich – Hook of Holland
corridors.notes: Overnight route; Stena requires foot passengers to book a cabin (no seat-only fare), which pushes the effective floor higher than pure deck-fare routes.

corridors.name: Helsinki – Stockholm
corridors.notes: Overnight cruise-ferry via the Aland archipelago; a reclining seat or cabin is effectively required. Cheapest online fares from ~EUR47-60.

corridors.name: Helsinki – Tallinn
corridors.notes: Busiest international ferry corridor in the world by some counts; 53+ sailings/week combined. Fastest crossing ~2h.

corridors.name: Heraklion – Santorini
corridors.notes: Fast catamaran 1h35-2h; conventional up to 4.5h. Runs year-round; typical economy EUR 110-130 in season, promos lower.

corridors.name: Hirtshals – Kristiansand
corridors.notes: LOWER CONFIDENCE: aggregator 'average ferry price' figures (~$250-330) appeared to be vehicle-inclusive; foot-passenger-only fares should be well under that, estimated here.

corridors.name: Holyhead – Dublin
corridors.notes: Two operators, up to 8 Stena sailings/day alone. Cheapest summer one-way seat quoted ~EUR42.

corridors.name: Hong Kong – Macau
corridors.notes: High-speed catamarans only, no vehicles. Weekend/night fares run higher (~HK$220 night, ~HK$310+ 'super/first' class). One of the world's busiest passenger-only ferry corridors.

corridors.name: Vancouver (Horseshoe Bay) – Nanaimo (Departure Bay)
corridors.notes: Adult fare CAD20 converted at CAD=0.72. Vehicle reservations now mandatory (drive-up no longer available) as of Oct 2025.

corridors.name: Hull – Rotterdam
corridors.notes: Overnight; deck fares from EUR14-19, but cabin-inclusive fares (effectively required for an overnight sailing) run GBP136-260 (~$175-335). Lo/hi here is deck-only; add ~$100+ for a cabin.

corridors.name: Hyannis – Nantucket
corridors.price_basis: adult passenger one-way (Steamship Authority traditional ferry lo, Hy-Line high-speed catamaran hi)
corridors.notes: CORRECTED 2026-07: original entry only priced Hy-Line's high-speed catamaran ($49-55, ~1h). Steamship Authority's own fare page confirms a cheaper traditional (non-high-speed) walk-on ferry at $21.50 one-way / $43 round-trip, 2h15m crossing, no reservation needed for foot passengers. duration_h=1 reflects the faster/more commonly chosen Hy-Line option; the $21.50 low end is on the slower 2.25h Steamship Authority boat, not the 1h boat -- this corridor genuinely has two different speed/price tiers, single duration_h field can't capture both.

corridors.name: Ibiza – Formentera
corridors.notes: One of the shortest, most frequent Med crossings -- departures roughly every 45 min in season. Relatively expensive per-km given tourist demand.

corridors.name: Incheon – Weihai
corridors.notes: 3 sailings/week (New Golden Bridge VII), overnight. One of several China-Korea Yellow Sea ferry links; Weidong also runs Qingdao-Incheon.

corridors.name: Kagoshima – Yakushima
corridors.notes: JPY12,700 one-way (2025 published fare, includes fuel adjustment). A slower, cheaper conventional car ferry ('Ferry Yakushima2') also exists on this route but isn't priced here.

corridors.name: Ketapang – Gilimanuk (Java–Bali Strait)
corridors.notes: State-subsidized 24-hour RoRo strait shuttle, IDR8,000 (~$0.50) adult fare -- by far the cheapest per-km corridor in this dataset, useful as a lower-bound anchor.

corridors.name: Kiel – Oslo
corridors.price_basis: adult foot passenger one-way, cabin berth required
corridors.notes: Color Line states fares start from EUR155/person -- no seat-only option on this ~20h crossing, so this is priced as a shared cabin berth, not a deck fare. Aggregator per-passenger averages ($1000+) appear to conflate car+cabin bundles and were discarded as unreliable.

corridors.name: Kos – Bodrum
corridors.notes: 30-min Aegean strait hop, 6 operators, strongly seasonal (much reduced Nov-Mar).

corridors.name: La Paz – Mazatlán
corridors.notes: Overnight Sea of Cortez crossing. Baja Ferries also runs the shorter, more frequent La Paz-Topolobampo route (~132 MXN-equivalent seat fare, not separately listed here).

corridors.name: Las Palmas – Santa Cruz de Tenerife
corridors.notes: Busiest inter-island Canaries corridor, ~11 sailings/day combined.

corridors.name: Liverpool – Belfast
corridors.notes: Day and night crossings both ~8h. Promotional foot-passenger sales have dipped as low as GBP19.

corridors.name: Livorno – Bastia
corridors.notes: Heavily seasonal, thin winter schedule. Cheapest advance fares from ~EUR23-26.

corridors.name: Málaga – Melilla
corridors.notes: Round-trip-no-vehicle average ~EUR114 cited by Balearia; halved and converted for a one-way estimate.

corridors.name: Manila – Cebu
corridors.price_basis: adult one-way, dorm bed to private cabin
corridors.notes: LOWER CONFIDENCE on both duration and frequency: sources described this as a ~22-24h overnight liner passage with 'weekly trips', which is ambiguous (could mean several sailings spread through the week, not literally once/week) -- check 2GO's live schedule before relying on this. Meals included in fare.

corridors.name: Melbourne (Geelong) – Devonport
corridors.notes: IMPORTANT: terminal moved from central Melbourne to the Port of Geelong (~1h from Melbourne CBD). Fares only sold as return (AUD380 winter / AUD660 summer); halved here to approximate one-way. Overnight Bass Strait crossing.

corridors.name: Mokpo – Jeju
corridors.notes: ~5 sailings/week; cheapest ~$23, average ~$50.

corridors.name: Naples – Palermo
corridors.notes: Overnight; deck/numbered-seat fares from ~EUR28-45, cabins extra.

corridors.name: Newhaven – Dieppe
corridors.notes: DFDS-only route since Transmanche closure years ago. 2-3 sailings/day rising to 4 in summer.

corridors.name: Nice – Bastia (Corsica)
corridors.notes: ~2 sailings/WEEK (seasonal) — too thin to treat as a dependable gateway; Toulon-Corsica runs year-round with more sailings.

corridors.name: North Sydney – Port aux Basques
corridors.notes: LOWER CONFIDENCE: the Canadian federal government cut Marine Atlantic fares 50% effective Aug 1 2025; sources disagree on whether cited figures (CAD43.78 vs a $13-81 range) are pre- or post-cut. Wide range reflects that uncertainty -- check marineatlantic.ca directly before use.

corridors.name: Osaka – Busan
corridors.notes: No seat-only option -- lowest fare is a shared inside-cabin bed (~KRW200,000), suites run to KRW750,000+ (~$465+). Roughly 3-4 sailings/week, not daily.

corridors.name: Phuket – Phi Phi
operators: Various (Phi Phi Cruiser, Chao Koh, Bangrong etc)
corridors.notes: THB450 base fare cited; excludes THB400 Phi Phi National Park entry fee charged on arrival. High aggregator averages ($81+) look inflated by bundled tour packages, not a plain ferry ticket.

corridors.name: Piombino – Portoferraio (Elba)
corridors.notes: Toremar quotes EUR12.32; Moby EUR17.70 for a single adult deck ticket -- among the cleanest confirmed fares in this dataset.

corridors.name: Piraeus – Heraklion (Crete)
corridors.notes: Year-round overnight workhorse route; fastest sailing 7h15m, conventional ~9h. Cabins from ~EUR60 extra.

corridors.name: Piraeus – Mykonos
corridors.notes: Conventional ferries 4h40-5h50; fastest catamarans 2h40. Economy numbered seat EUR52-53.

corridors.name: Piraeus – Rhodes
corridors.notes: Long Dodecanese overnight run (10h40 fastest to 17h43 slowest). A limited 'super economy' promo fare of EUR20 is sometimes available.

corridors.name: Piraeus – Santorini
corridors.notes: Frequency drops sharply Nov-Mar. Conventional Blue Star ~EUR60 vs high-speed SeaJets catamaran EUR75-110+.

corridors.name: Playa del Carmen – Cozumel
corridors.notes: Two competing passenger-only operators, departures roughly every 30 min. Winjet ~$14, Ultramar ~$17-20.

corridors.name: Plymouth – Roscoff
corridors.notes: 5.5-8h daytime, 9-13h overnight sailings on same route. Up to 7 sailings/week (not daily).

corridors.name: Port Angeles – Victoria
corridors.notes: VERIFIED via direct fetch: adult walk-on fare is $25.00 USD (fuel surcharge included). Runs year-round with more sailings in summer than winter.

corridors.name: Portsmouth – Caen (Ouistreham)
corridors.notes: Duration 4.5-7.5h depending on sailing (day vs overnight). Aggregator quoted range up to $350 for peak/cabin bookings, trimmed here.

corridors.name: Portsmouth – Santander
corridors.notes: MODERATE CONFIDENCE on duration: two independent aggregator snippets and the Brittany Ferries crossings-page title both said 32-33.5h, which is long for the distance -- kept as sourced but flagged. Only 2 sailings/week. Foot passengers on this multi-day overnight crossing effectively need a cabin; fare reflects that.

corridors.name: Portsmouth – St Malo
corridors.notes: Long crossing (~11.5h), often overnight; foot passenger fares from GBP49 cited, average nearer GBP169-239 once cabin included. Not daily.

corridors.name: Puttgarden – Rødby
corridors.notes: Turn-up-and-go shuttle every 30 min, 24/7/365. Foot-passenger tickets are terminal-only, not sold online. Being partly superseded by the Fehmarnbelt tunnel (under construction).

corridors.name: Rhodes – Marmaris
corridors.notes: Fast catamaran, seasonal (roughly Apr-Oct). Several small Turkish operators, prices similar across them.

corridors.name: Rosslare – Cherbourg
corridors.notes: Direct Ireland-France route bypassing the UK land-bridge; ~19h overnight, cabin typically needed, only 4 sailings/week.

corridors.name: Rostock – Gedser
corridors.notes: Pedestrian fare EUR14 low season / EUR28 high season, quoted directly by Scandlines.

corridors.name: Seattle – Bainbridge Island
corridors.price_basis: adult walk-on, WSF's round-trip-equivalent fare (see notes)
corridors.notes: VERIFIED via direct fetch of the official WSDOT fare page: adult walk-on is $11.35, but WSF collects it only westbound (Seattle-departing) and the Bainbridge-to-Seattle leg is free -- so a one-way Seattle-to-Bainbridge trip costs $11.35 and the reverse costs $0. Unique to WSF's central-Sound routes.

corridors.name: Seattle – Victoria (Clipper)
corridors.price_basis: adult economy one-way, passenger-only fast ferry
corridors.notes: Passenger-only catamaran, 1-3/day varying by season; highly dynamic pricing (sources conflict $115 RT promo vs $120-200 one-way) — medium confidence band.

corridors.name: Singapore (HarbourFront) – Batam
corridors.notes: Ticket price EXCLUDES a S$10 Singapore departure fee and an Rp100,000 (~$10) Batam terminal fee charged separately at each end -- true door-to-door cost is roughly double the base fare.

corridors.name: Split – Ancona
corridors.notes: Overnight; schedule thins sharply outside summer season. Deck fare EUR56-82 cited.

corridors.name: St Martin (Marigot) – Anguilla (Blowing Point)
corridors.notes: $30/EUR30 fare does NOT include a EUR7 passenger head-fee at the Marigot ticket office or Anguilla port taxes (US$11-28 depending on stay length) -- those are add-ons, not part of the ferry ticket itself.

corridors.name: Tallinn – Stockholm
corridors.notes: Cruise ferry (Baltic Queen), sails every other evening from Tallinn year-round with extra summer departures on Romantika.

corridors.name: Tarifa – Tanger Ville
corridors.notes: Balearia took over the FRS concession May 2025; ~15 daily sailings combined with AML. Fast ferry into central Tangier.

corridors.name: Trinidad – Tobago
operators: TTIT (Trinidad and Tobago Inter-Island Transportation Co.)
corridors.notes: State-run fast ferry, TT$75 economy one-way (~$11 at TTD=6.75/USD peg). Crossing 2.5-5h depending on vessel.

corridors.name: Vancouver (Tsawwassen) – Victoria (Swartz Bay)
corridors.notes: Adult fare CAD18.50-20 converted at CAD=0.72 USD. BC seniors ride free Mon-Thu.

corridors.name: Turku – Stockholm
corridors.notes: Overnight via Aland. Cheap cabin berths from EUR37.50 (Viking) seen in low season.

corridors.name: Valencia – Ibiza
corridors.notes: Fast-craft option ~5h10m with Balearia; slower conventional sailings also exist.

corridors.name: Villa San Giovanni – Messina
corridors.notes: Pedestrian ticket EUR2.50 quoted directly by the operator; a 20-minute strait shuttle running ~30x/day. Very cheap per-km -- a useful floor datapoint.

corridors.name: Wellington – Picton
corridors.notes: Cook Strait crossing; two competing operators (NZD60 Interislander, NZD68 Bluebridge cheapest foot-passenger fares).

corridors.name: Woods Hole – Martha's Vineyard
corridors.notes: Confirmed directly by operator: $11 one-way / $22 round-trip. No vehicle reservation required for foot passengers; very high frequency in season.

corridors.name: Ystad – Rønne (Bornholm)
corridors.notes: Standard adult ticket from DKK99. Wide aggregator range ($16-423) trimmed to a typical band.
